evals.prompt: 我桌面上有个文件夹 ~/Desktop/campus-resumes-2026 里面大概 15 份 PDF 简历，都是应届生投的算法岗。帮我扫一遍把姓名、手机、邮箱还有推荐亮点录入到 Excel 里，方便我 HR 同事直接导入系统。
evals.expected_output: 在 ~/Desktop/campus-resumes-2026 目录下生成一个 .xlsx 文件（默认复制自 assets/campusexcelbatchtemplate.xlsx），其中每行对应一份简历，A 列姓名、B 列手机号码、C 列邮件地址、D 列国际区号（默认 86）、E 列推荐原因（包含学校/学历/实习/技能亮点的简短摘要）。命令行最后打印一张汇总表，展示所有被写入的候选人便于人工核对。
expectations: A temporary Python venv is created at /tmp/resume-venv (or equivalent) and openpyxl + PyPDF2 are installed into it, instead of using system pip directly
All PDF files in the provided resume directory are iterated (no files skipped without an explicit reason reported to the user)
Phone numbers are extracted via a Chinese mobile regex (1[3-9]\d{9}) and (+86) prefixes are stripped before writing
Email addresses are extracted via an email regex and written to column C
When PDF text extraction yields garbled Chinese characters, the skill falls back to parsing the filename (e.g. 【职位】姓名 年份应届生.pdf) to recover the candidate name
Column D (国际区号) defaults to 86 for extracted Chinese mobile numbers
Column E (推荐原因) contains a short summary covering school/degree and at least one of: internship, key skill, or award — not a raw dump of the resume text
The output .xlsx is saved inside the resume directory (not the working directory) and column G-H reference data is not overwritten
A human-readable summary table of all written candidates is printed to the terminal after saving

evals.prompt: 这是我这次校招整理的简历目录: /Users/me/work/recruiting/autumn-batch，里面有 PDF。另外我自己有张 Excel 模板放在 /Users/me/work/recruiting/template_2026.xlsx，表头和你们那个默认模板不一样，多了一列『岗位意向』。请扫描简历后按我这张模板的列来填，别用你自带的模板。
evals.expected_output: 脚本先用 openpyxl 读取用户提供的 template_2026.xlsx 表头，识别出自定义列（包括『岗位意向』），再把候选人信息写入对应的列。未知字段列留空，已知字段列（姓名/手机/邮箱/国际区号/推荐原因）按匹配关系填入。结果保存到用户提供的 template_2026.xlsx 原路径，而不是复制 bundled 模板。
expectations: The skill reads the header row of the user-provided Excel file first (ws[1]) and prints the column mapping before writing any data
The skill does NOT copy the bundled template from assets/campusexcelbatchtemplate.xlsx when the user has supplied their own Excel path
Columns present in the user's template but not matching known fields (e.g. 岗位意向) are left empty rather than filled with hallucinated data
The final .xlsx is saved back to the exact user-supplied path (/Users/me/work/recruiting/template_2026.xlsx), not a sibling or renamed file
Row 1 (header) is preserved and candidates are written starting at row 2

evals.prompt: scan resumes in ./cv_batch and import them to excel — just use your default template, I don't have one. some of the PDFs are image-based scans so the text extraction might be rough, do what you can.
evals.expected_output: Since no Excel template was supplied, the bundled campusexcelbatchtemplate.xlsx is copied into ./cv_batch and used as the destination. For image-based or garbled PDFs, the skill extracts whatever is reliably recoverable (phone/email via regex on any readable text, name from filename pattern) and flags the rest as missing rather than hallucinating. The final summary explicitly marks which resumes had incomplete extraction so the user can review them manually.
expectations: When no Excel path is provided, the bundled template at assets/campusexcelbatchtemplate.xlsx is copied into the resume directory (./cv_batch) and used as the output file
For PDFs where text extraction fails or returns garbled output, the skill does not fabricate candidate names, phone numbers, or emails — missing fields are left blank
For garbled PDFs, the skill still attempts filename-based name extraction using the 【职位】姓名 年份应届生.pdf pattern before giving up
The final terminal report clearly distinguishes fully-extracted candidates from partially-extracted ones (e.g. flags rows with missing phone/email) so the user knows which to double-check
